title: The Darkness Before Them
author: Matthew Ward
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Darkness Before Them" book by Matthew Ward?
Options:
A. Bathed in starlight and dotted by leaping watch-flames from torch house minarets, Tyzanta possessed an unlikely beauty. Over the centuries, strata upon strata of new buildings had been raised atop those that had come before, buried in turn as the wealthy sought escape from squalor. The broad-based spire towered over the crumbling tenements and bow-roofed warehouses of the Undertown slums, honeycombed with forgotten streets and encrusted with mansions, temples, ramshackle townhouses and overgrown gardens, all served in turn by tiered canals and spiralling roadways, and supported by a precarious tangle of archways, buttresses and struts that seemed always on the point of collapse. 
B. Illuminated by starlight and dotted with leaping watch-fire lights from minaret torch houses, Tyzanta possessed an unlikely beauty. Over time, layer after layer of new buildings were constructed atop previous ones, buried in turn as the wealthy tried to get away from poverty. The broad-based spire towered over the crumbling tenements and bow-roofed warehouses of the Undertown slums, honeycombed with forgotten streets and covered with mansions, temples, rickety townhouses and overgrown gardens, all served one after the other by tiered canals and winding roadways, and supported by a precarious tangle of archways, buttresses and struts that appeared always on the verge of collapse.
C. Illuminated by starlight and dotted with leaping watch-fire lights from minaret torch houses, Tyzanta had an unlikely beauty. Over time, layers upon layers of new buildings were constructed on top of previous ones, buried in turn as the affluent tried to escape poverty. The broad-spired tower overlooked the crumbling tenements and bow-roofed warehouses of the Undertown slums, honeycombed with forgotten streets and encrusted with mansions, temples, rickety townhouses and overgrown gardens, all served by tiered canals and spiraling roadways in succession, and supported by a precarious tangle of archways, buttresses and struts that appeared perpetually on the verge of collapse.
D. Bathed in starshine and marked by jumping watch-flames from minaret torch houses, Tyzanta had an improbable loveliness. As the wealthy sought escape from destitution, new buildings were repeatedly raised atop those constructed earlier over the centuries, buried in turn. The wide-based spire towered above the dilapidated tenements and bow-roofed warehouses of the Undertown slums, honeycombed with forgotten streets and encrusted with mansions, temples, ramshackle townhouses and overgrown gardens, all served one after the other by tiered canals and winding roadways, and supported by a precarious tangle of archways, buttresses and struts that seemed always about to give way.
Answer:
